title: uragoga violacea
side_effects: Uragoga violacea can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting, which can be severe in some cases. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant's extracts may lead to headaches, fatigue, and insomnia. In rare instances, uragoga violacea can cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and swelling, especially for individuals with sensitive skin or allergies. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using uragoga violacea to ensure safe and effective use.
